title: Root Insurance Turned Profitable in 2025 and the Market Hasn't Caught Up Yet
text: Root, Inc. (NASDAQ:ROOT) has reached a significant turning point, posting full-year 2025 net income of $40.3 million, a 30% year-over-year increase on $1.5 billion in premiums. More importantly, the company achieved a sub-100% combined ratio, marking its first year of underwriting profitability. The independent agent channel emerged as Root's fastest-growing segment, with new writings more than tripled year-over-year in Q3 2025 within a market the company values above $100 billion. The company's AI-driven pricing capabilities also improved, delivering a 20%+ boost in estimated customer lifetime values. Despite these achievements, ROOT stock has declined 8.2% over the past week and roughly 21% year-to-date. However, Reddit sentiment scores the stock at a bullish 80 out of 100, suggesting investors following the company believe the market hasn't fully recognized its progress. Management has signaled that planned investments and higher loss ratios will likely moderate 2026 net income. Analysts currently rate ROOT a Buy with a consensus price target of $108.40, implying approximately 87% upside, though validation of this outlook depends on whether the independent agent channel continues scaling while maintaining profitability.
